Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: Find the capital letter that says /a/.
Explanation: A is the capital shape for the letter a.
LearnBot Tip: Capital letters are tall letter signs.
Voice Script: Look at each card. Tap the big A because it names the /a/ sound.
Visual Description: Four letter cards with A highlighted after the answer.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: Which card shows small b?
Explanation: Small b has a tall line and a round tummy.
LearnBot Tip: Trace the letter shape with your eyes.
Voice Script: Find small b. It has one tall stick and one round part.
Visual Description: Four cards showing B b h p with small b highlighted after the answer.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: After C, what letter do we say next?
Explanation: The alphabet goes A, B, C, then D.
LearnBot Tip: Say the alphabet softly before choosing.
Voice Script: Say A, B, C. The next letter you hear is D.
Visual Description: A short alphabet path A B C D with D glowing after the answer.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: The letter before T is hiding. Which one is it?
Instructions: Choose the letter that comes just before T.
Explanation: Alphabet order places S right before T.
LearnBot Tip: Move one step back on the alphabet path.
Voice Script: Look near T. The letter just before it is S.
Visual Description: A mini path R S T with S highlighted as the previous letter.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: M has a lowercase partner. Tap it.
Explanation: Uppercase M and lowercase m share the same letter name.
LearnBot Tip: Match the name first, then the size.
Voice Script: M can look big or small. The small partner is m.
Visual Description: Large M beside four lowercase choices with m highlighted after the answer.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: p needs its capital letter. Which one matches?
Explanation: P is the capital form that matches lowercase p.
LearnBot Tip: Turn the little letter into its big shape.
Voice Script: Look at p. Its capital partner is P.
Visual Description: Lowercase p points to uppercase options P R K B with P highlighted.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: Pair each capital letter with its small letter.
Correct Answer: A = a; B = b; C = c
Explanation: Each capital letter pairs with the same lowercase letter name.
LearnBot Tip: Start with one easy pair, then continue.
Voice Script: Match A with a, B with b, and C with c.
Visual Description: Three uppercase cards and three lowercase cards arranged for pairing.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: Help the forest signs find their lowercase partners.
Correct Answer: M = m; S = s; T = t
Explanation: The big and small signs keep the same letter names.
LearnBot Tip: Say the letter name before you pair.
Voice Script: Find M with m. Then match S with s and T with t.
Visual Description: Forest sign cards showing M S T and lowercase m s t for matching.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: Match the leaf cards with the same letters.
Instructions: Pair the uppercase and lowercase cards.
Correct Answer: P = p; R = r; N = n
Explanation: Letters can change size but keep their names.
LearnBot Tip: Look for the same sound in each pair.
Voice Script: Pair P with p, R with r, and N with n.
Visual Description: Leaf cards with P R N on one side and p r n on the other.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: Join each alphabet card to its tiny match.
Correct Answer: D = d; E = e; F = f
Explanation: Capital D, E, and F match their small forms.
LearnBot Tip: Check the letter name, not the colour.
Voice Script: Match D to d, E to e, and F to f.
Visual Description: Three bright alphabet cards D E F with lowercase d e f cards below.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: These small letters need capital partners.
Instructions: Match each lowercase letter to uppercase.
Correct Answer: h = H; l = L; o = O
Explanation: The lowercase letters h, l, and o match H, L, and O.
LearnBot Tip: Small letters can still find their capitals.
Voice Script: Match h with H, l with L, and o with O.
Visual Description: Lowercase h l o cards connect to uppercase H L O cards.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: Tap capital B.
Explanation: Capital B has one tall line and two round bumps.
LearnBot Tip: Look for the card with two bumps.
Voice Script: Find the capital B card. Tap it when you see the two round parts.
Visual Description: Four uppercase cards with B highlighted after a correct tap.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: Touch the small s card.
Explanation: Lowercase s is the small curvy letter.
LearnBot Tip: Uppercase S is taller than small s.
Voice Script: Look for the smaller curvy s. Tap that card.
Visual Description: Cards S s c z with lowercase s highlighted after the answer.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: Which lowercase card matches capital T?
Explanation: Capital T matches lowercase t because they share a name.
LearnBot Tip: Listen to the letter name T.
Voice Script: Capital T needs small t. Choose the matching letter name.
Visual Description: Capital T beside lowercase choices f t l i with t highlighted.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: Start at A. Tap the next letter.
Explanation: A moves forward one step to B.
LearnBot Tip: Next means go forward one place.
Voice Script: Start at A and take one step forward. You land on B.
Visual Description: A simple A to B arrow with B highlighted after the tap.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: N has a neighbour before it. Tap that letter.
Explanation: M comes just before N in alphabet order.
LearnBot Tip: Before means step backward once.
Voice Script: Look at L, M, N. The letter before N is M.
Visual Description: A mini path L M N O with M highlighted as before N.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: Complete the alphabet path: A _ C
Explanation: A, B, C is the beginning of the alphabet path.
LearnBot Tip: Fill the middle sound you hear.
Voice Script: Say A, B, C. The missing letter is B.
Visual Description: Three stepping stones A blank C with B appearing after the answer.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: What letter completes R _ T?
Explanation: R, S, T keeps the letters in order.
LearnBot Tip: Look between the two letters.
Voice Script: Say R, S, T. S belongs in the empty space.
Visual Description: Sequence cards R blank T with S highlighted in the middle.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: Fill the lowercase trail: m _ o
Explanation: m, n, o follows the same alphabet order.
LearnBot Tip: Lowercase letters follow the same path.
Voice Script: Read the lowercase trail m, n, o. The answer is n.
Visual Description: Lowercase letter trail m blank o with n appearing after the response.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: The alphabet ends X _ Z. What is missing?
Explanation: X, Y, Z are the final three alphabet letters.
LearnBot Tip: Y sits between X and Z.
Voice Script: At the end of the alphabet we say X, Y, Z.
Visual Description: Final alphabet cards X blank Z with Y glowing in the blank.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: Type the small partner for A.
Explanation: The lowercase partner for capital A is a.
LearnBot Tip: Use the little shape of the same letter.
Voice Script: Type small a. It matches the capital A.
Visual Description: Capital A with an empty answer box for lowercase a.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.

Explanation: The capital partner for lowercase m is M.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.

Explanation: The alphabet moves from P to Q.
LearnBot Tip: Forward one step gives the next letter.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: Write the letter just before E.
Explanation: D is the letter that comes before E.

Voice Script: Before E, we say D. Type D in the box.
Visual Description: Alphabet tiles D E with D highlighted as the previous letter.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: Type the lowercase match for T.
Explanation: Capital T and lowercase t are the same letter name.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: A and a name the same letter.
Explanation: A and a are matching forms of one letter.
LearnBot Tip: Big and small forms can belong together.

Visual Description: A large A and small a connected with a matching line.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: B and d are matching letters.
Explanation: B matches b, but d is a different letter.
LearnBot Tip: Check both the name and the shape.
Voice Script: B does not match d. B matches small b.
Visual Description: Capital B and lowercase d shown apart with a gentle no-match marker.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: C comes before D in the alphabet.
Explanation: Alphabet order says C first, then D.
LearnBot Tip: Read the alphabet from left to right.
Voice Script: C comes before D. That statement is true.
Visual Description: A short alphabet strip C D with an arrow moving from C to D.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: T comes right before S.
Explanation: S appears before T, so the sentence is false.
LearnBot Tip: Use the alphabet path to check order.
Voice Script: Look at S, then T. T does not come before S.
Visual Description: Alphabet strip S T with S shown before T to correct the statement.

Subject: English
Curriculum Alignment: KSSR Year 1 English: recognise and name letters of the alphabet and connect letters to early reading.
Cambridge Alignment: Cambridge Primary English Stage 1: identify letters, print concepts, and simple alphabet knowledge.
Question: M and m are a matching pair.
Explanation: M and m are capital and lowercase forms of M.
LearnBot Tip: Same letter name means they match.
Voice Script: M and m go together. One is big and one is small.
Visual Description: Capital M and lowercase m inside a matching pair frame.
